xposts.title: Ruliad as all finite automata rules applied unboundedly
xposts.excerpt: The Ruliad is the result of the simultaneous application of all finite automata rules for an unbounded number of steps. The universe we observe looks exactly as it would if we were in the Ruliad.

xposts.title: Nondeterministic Turing Machines for foundational physics
xposts.excerpt: Here is the recording of my conversation with @stephen_wolfram, on understanding Nondeterministic Turing Machines as a paradigm for foundational physics and mental representations

xposts.title: Mind and universe as automata: Wolfram conversation
xposts.excerpt: What kind of automata are mind and universe? A conversation with Stephen Wolfram

xposts.title: Turing complete cellular automaton vs Python for universe
xposts.excerpt: A Turing complete cellular automaton is not better or worse than Python for running integer arithmetic, a mind or a universe.

xposts.title: Ruliad as ultimate tautology implementing universe
xposts.excerpt: I think the ruliad is implemented by default, it’s the ultimate tautology

terms.title: Ruliad
terms.definition: The result of applying all finite automata rules simultaneously for unbounded steps; the space containing all possible computations from which our observed universe emerges as a branch.
